title: cheirolophus massonianus
intro: Cheirolophus massonianus, also known as the Madeira woolly plant, is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family, native to the island of Madeira. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and gastrointestinal problems due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Cheirolophus massonianus include its ability to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and bronchitis, as well as its use as a natural remedy for digestive issues and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Cheirolophus massonianus often include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
